blockers: Registry serving metadata did not have a machine-checkable advisory-only source contract.

registry trio persistence is fail-loud after partial write, not transactional rollback

subagent_chain.summary: Designed advisory-only registry_serving_contract_v1 with PIT, feature/schema, policy, q10/q50/q90 artifact hash, and no-authority boundaries.
Implemented Python registry_serving_contract.py, model_registry integration, and tests.
Implemented Rust registry advisory serving metadata validation groundwork.
Highlighted persistence atomicity and contract hardening concerns for remediation.
Accepted advisory/no-authority ML contract direction after initial remediation.
Found missing Rust row artifact_sha256 to contract hash comparison and Python/Rust extra-field canonicalization mismatch.
Closed Python schema and persistence concerns: unknown top-level fields invalid and partial trio persistence raises registry_trio_persistence_incomplete.
Closed Rust quantile artifact comparison concern by binding resolved artifact_sha256 to contract artifact_hashes for the resolved slot.
Made Rust get_build_capabilities report reload_edge_predictor=false with registry_authorized_reload_required=true.
Made FastAPI capability aggregation keep reload_edge_predictor=false even when stale IPC claims true, and surfaced capability metadata.
Closed FastAPI capability concern; noted only stale nearby Rust comments as non-blocking cleanup.
Regression passed: py_compile, focused Python 58, expanded ML 106/13 skipped, rustfmt, Rust registry 25, reload 4, capabilities 1, and diff-check.
Accepted WP3 as source-contract ready with residual future-work concerns around runtime integration, transactional rollback, and promotion-serving readiness.

artifact_delta: new machine-checkable advisory-only registry serving contract, Python registry attachment and hash binding, Rust row artifact hash validation, and fail-closed reload capability reporting
test_delta: new focused registry serving contract and model registry tests plus Rust registry/reload/capability tests passed
proof_delta: registry advisory metadata now requires PIT-derived serving parity hashes and row-bound q10/q50/q90 artifact hashes before it can be attached or validated
next_action_quality: WP4 advisory DreamEngine role hardening is eligible as source-only work, with LLM/advisory outputs constrained to inactive review packets and no mutation authority
concerns: registry trio DB persistence fails loud on partial write but is not transactionally rolled back in this source-only cycle
runtime DB JSONB extraction and authorized serving reload integration remain future gated work
promotion_serving_ready remains false; WP3 does not authorize model promotion or serving reload
nearby Rust comments still contain stale ORT-readiness wording, while executable capabilities and tests are fail-closed
